what_it_does: This file documents a verified issue where roughly fifty cross-scheme edges point at address schemes that have no corresponding vault file, causing dangling/unresolved nodes in Obsidian's graph, and it lists three options for handling the situation without choosing one.
observations: About 50 verified edges target decision://, image://, code://, session://, and ui:// addresses with no vault file.
In Obsidian these edges render as dangling or unresolved link stubs.
Three options are surfaced: leave edges as plain text, generate thin stub notes per external target, or build resolver-backed embeds for image/blob assets.
The stub-note option is framed as naturally pairing with future image:// and code:// lens work.
The item links to board://item-4baaab55 via a part_of relation and is filed in the dragnet-development channel.
It explicitly maps the issue onto a 'one walkable graph' ambition where the vault graph is a partial render of a richer address graph.
standouts: The cross-scheme edges are treated as evidence that the address graph is richer than any single host can natively display.
The resolver-backed embed option proposes turning dangling image edges into viewable Obsidian embeds via an HTTP serve route.
The item deliberately refuses to decide, framing the options as a design tension to preserve.
notes: A verified design note that diagnoses dangling cross-scheme graph edges, counts them, and lays out three unresolved strategies tied to the broader walkable-graph goal.
questions: How exactly were the ~50 edges counted and from which source items?
What is the specific content of 'doc-#1' referenced as the 'one walkable graph' ambition?
What criteria or owner will eventually decide among the three options?
What is the planned timeline or dependency chain for the future image:// and code:// vault representation work?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is to surface the dangling-node problem and candidate options without deciding, and the file does exactly that.
apparent_themes: future extensibility for media and code lenses
boundary between address graph and host-native graph
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make the team conscious that unresolved cross-scheme links are not merely cosmetic but a structural boundary between the richer address graph and Obsidian's partial vault view, while keeping the strategic options open.
novelty: It reframes Obsidian unresolved links as a positive indicator of an address graph that exceeds vault scope, and proposes resolver-backed embeds as a way to bridge that gap for image assets.
contribution: It preserves a precise diagnosis of the dangling-node problem, a quantitative signal (~50 edges), and a set of candidate strategies, so a fused system can later query the tradeoff space and the relationship to the walkable-graph ambition.
summary_for_embedding: A current dragnet-development noticeboard item that records a verified dangling-node problem in which roughly fifty cross-scheme edges reference decision://, image://, code://, session://, and ui:// addresses that lack vault files, and lists three unresolved handling options—leave as text, generate stub notes, or use resolver-backed embeds—within the context of a single walkable graph vision.
intention_for_embedding: It was filed to surface the cross-scheme edge problem and its possible resolutions without deciding among them.
